groups.context_description: Term names a visceral physical expression that carries the inner state of grief and anguish at communal devastation
Term names the manifest presence of God's glory as an encounter that human beings experience — producing awe, prostration, worship, and inability to stand
Term names the human inner act of directing honour and glory toward God — the worship orientation expressed in ascription, declaration, and praise
Term names the inner self or whole being of the person as the seat of praise, dignity, and orientation — the "glory" as the locus of personal inner identity before God
Term names human honour as the outer expression of inner character — wisdom, humility, and fear of the Lord as the source from which honour flows
Term names human honour as a relational-social condition of dignity and worth — received from God, threatened by others, lost in judgment, or exchanged for lesser things
Term names the volitional or divinely imposed hardening of the heart — the capacity for responsive obedience becomes weighted/resistant toward God
Term describes the moral gravity of sin or iniquity as a weight that is borne by the person or community
Term names the dulling or weighting of the inner faculty of spiritual hearing and understanding
Term measures the inner weight of suffering, anguish, or divine discipline experienced by the person
Term names the volitional closing of the inner faculty of spiritual hearing — the person deliberately makes themselves unresponsive to God's word
Term describes the splendour or magnificence that accompanies a person's position or status — framing the conditions in which inner relational orientation toward God is expressed
Term names material richness or splendour as the setting in which inner spiritual orientation — faithfulness or its corruption toward God — is enacted or exposed
Term names the hardening of the inner being — the heart or mind rendered impervious to spiritual perception, understanding, and responsiveness to God
Term names the state of hardening — the condition of the inner being rendered insensible to God, expressed as hardness of heart producing spiritual blindness and alienation
Term names the proud heart — the inner-being of self-exaltation that leads to unfaithfulness, destruction, or divine judgment
Term names God's exalted inner-being and his sovereign reversals — the divine transcendence and the justice by which the high are brought low and the low are lifted
Term names pride-of-heart as an inner-being condition leading to destruction — the height of self-regard that draws God's judgment
Term names God's transcendent inner-being height — the majesty of God that exceeds all human reach and comprehension
Term names human haughtiness as an inner-being condition that will be humbled when God alone is exalted — the pride of the human person brought to nothing before divine majesty
Term names pride as an inner-being condition subject to divine humbling — the self-exaltation that God alone can bring low
Term names arrogance/presumption as an inner-being disposition that deceives, produces strife, and leads to downfall — the pride of heart that sets itself above God and others
Term names the pride of the hardened heart — the inner condition of self-exaltation that leads directly to divine removal of glory and authority
Term names God's transcendent position on high — from which he acts, rescues, and pours out his Spirit on the lowly; the height of God as the origin of his inner-being engagement with the world
Term names human pride expressed as height — the arrogant inner orientation that reaches upward against God or others, and is brought low
Term names the assertive inner condition — ranging from urgent pleading, to the strengthened soul, to insolent self-assertion over others
Term names the cosmic defeat of Rahab — God's inner-being of power and understanding expressed in the crushing of the primordial chaos-monster, invoked as the ground of trust
Term names the proud person — the inner orientation of self-reliance that leads away from God
Term names the pride/span of human life — the brief arc of toil and trouble that characterises the inner-being experience of mortal existence
Term names pride as a defining character-category — sons of pride as those whose inner orientation of self-assertion makes them subject to powerful forces
Term names legitimate boasting — the inner-being orientation of glorying in God, grace, weakness, and the cross; the proper direction of human boasting
Term names illegitimate boasting — the inner orientation of pride that asserts itself in law, flesh, or outward appearance rather than in God
Term names Paul's paradoxical boasting in weakness — the sustained inner-being act of turning normal pride on its head, boasting of what brings shame in order to reveal divine power
Term names the ground of legitimate boasting — what one may rightly claim before God or others, and the contrast between human and divine standards
Term names the corrupted boasting that undermines community — pride in the face of sin
Term names the apostolic and legitimate boasting — the inner-being stance of Paul and communities who can stand with clear conscience before God and before one another
Term names the arrogant/presumptuous person — the inner-being orientation of self-assertion against God and others, subject to divine rebuke and shame
Term names the inner act of presumptuous defiance — the wilful, boiling inner-being choice to override God's command or act arrogantly toward him and others
Term names the state of being puffed up with conceit — an inner-being inflation that produces spiritual blindness, disordered desires, and opposition to God
Term names pride as an inner-being condition arising from the heart — one of the evils that proceed from within the person and defile them
Term names arrogant boasting as an inner-being disposition — the pride that asserts itself against God, arising from the world rather than the Father
Term names the arrogant person — whose inner-being orientation of pride draws divine opposition and whose heart devises proud thoughts
Term names rebellion as an inner-being condition of the community before God — the persistent, characterising orientation of wilful defiance, stubbornness, and unwillingness to hear God's word
Term names the barely-surviving inner condition — the smoldering wick that God will not quench, disclosing his compassion toward the fragile inner life
Term names the braggart as an inner-being character type — the person whose inner orientation is self-inflation, listed among vices that mark spiritual corruption
Term names the human heart being lifted up — the inner-being of self-exaltation, pride, or forgetfulness of God, which leads to judgment or collapse
Term names God exalting persons or his people — the inner-being of divine favour expressed in raising up the humble or the anointed
Term names the doxological exaltation of God — the inner-being act of worship that raises God's name and glory above all
Term names the inner-being consequence of exaltation — divine reversal, justice, or the restoration of dignity
Term names the contribution that arises from the stirred heart and moving spirit — the inner-being act of giving to God from personal devotion
Term names the contribution as the condition of divine blessing and the measure of faithfulness — giving to God as the inner-being orientation of covenant loyalty
Term names the height of human pride — the arrogant heart and haughty eyes that will be humbled, and the inscrutability of the royal heart
Term names the lofty place of misdirected worship — the physical expression of Israel's inner spiritual prostitution, built in every street as a monument to unfaithfulness
Term names the lifted heart — pride, forgetfulness of God, self-exaltation
Term names God exalting persons
Term names doxological exaltation of God
Term names divine reversal and justice through exaltation
Term names haughty walking as an expression of inner pride — the outer posture that discloses an inward arrogance now under judgment
Term names the uplifting of God — the rising of divine majesty before which nations scatter
Term names the rising/triumphing of God — the inner-being expression of divine power in victory
Term names human self-elevation and its danger — the rising up of the person before God and the divine response
Term names pride as the inner-being condition that brings humbling — acknowledged in suffering, concealed by God in mercy, and lamented by the prophet's soul
Term names insolent fury — the inner-being condition of violent, arrogant rage exercised against the vulnerable, and celebrated when it ceases
Term names arrogance as an inner-being disorder — an inflation of self that produces relational breakdown
Term names the inner-being inflation of conceit — being puffed up with knowledge, pride, or sensuous reasoning, in contrast to love which builds
Term names the proud inner character — the self-elevated disposition of pride that expresses itself in arrogance and empty boasting
Term names the proud person as an inner-being type — characterised by arrogance and contempt, and subject to divine abasement
Term names pride as the object of God's inner hatred — the inner-being disposition that stands opposed to the fear of the Lord
Term names pride as a defining inner condition — the self-exalting disposition of the wicked, worn like a garment, arising from the heart, and subject to divine judgment
Term names divine majesty/triumph — the same root applied positively to God's greatness and Israel's victory through God
Term names human pride as an inner-being condition that leads to destruction — the arrogance that makes a nation or person stumble, and that God specifically abhors, spoils, or brings low
Term names the divine majesty — the inner greatness of God expressed in judgment, creation, and the restoration of his people
Term names divine majesty — the inner-being greatness of God robed in sovereign power, before which human arrogance fails
Term names human pride as an inner-being excess — the arrogant speech and proud posture of those who set themselves above others
Term names revolt as an inner disposition of rebellion against God — a deep turning away expressed in false teaching, corrupted speech, and covenantal betrayal
Term names transgression against God as an inner act of covenantal rebellion — the person's inner disposition of defiance and its acknowledgment in confession or condemnation
Term names rebellion against human authority as an inner disposition of political defiance — the wilful choice to resist or break from established rule
Term names rebellion as an inner act of hardening — the wilful resistance to God's voice expressed as a hardened heart refusing to respond
Term names rebellion against God as an inner act of wilful defiance — the deliberate rejection of covenantal obligation expressed as turning away, hardening, and refusing to submit
Term names rebellion against human authority as an inner act of will — the deliberate choice to resist political authority, involving inner resolve and calculated intent
Term names rebellion as an inner act of wilful breach against the Lord — the covenantal heart-posture of defiance acknowledged as a possibility and solemnly repudiated
Term names rebellion as the characteristic inner disposition of a community in persistent defiance of authority
Term names transgression as the inner moral burden of guilt and rebellion against God — the state of having broken covenant, requiring confession, forgiveness, or bearing the weight of judgment

Term names transgression as an inner character disposition — the quality of a person or community whose inner orientation is persistently toward sin, expressing itself in patterns of relational and moral failure
Term names apostasy as an inner act of covenantal abandonment — the deliberate turning away from foundational identity and commitment

Term names dispute and hostility as expressions of inner rebellion or occasions for inner endurance — endurance against hostility guards against inner weariness, and rebellion names an inner disposition of defiance
Term names the removal of idols and objects of false worship as an inner act of reorientation — putting away foreign gods is an expression of turning the heart back to God

Term names the removal or loss of inner-being realities — God's steadfast love withdrawn, the heart of stone removed, guilt and iniquity taken away — describing divine action on or loss of inner-being blessings
Term names the moral-volitional act of putting away inner dispositions and corrupting elements — vexation, false ways, crooked speech, evil, and perversity actively removed from the inner person
Term names the inner act of turning aside from God's way — the volitional departure from covenantal faithfulness expressed as apostasy, persistent sinfulness, or rebellion of the heart
Term names the inner disposition of faithfulness expressed by not turning aside — remaining on the path of God's commands as a sustained inner commitment
Term names the inner-responsive act of turning aside toward God or his revelation — directing attention and presence toward divine encounter
Term names the departure or withdrawal of God's Spirit, presence, face, or steadfast love — the inner-relational consequence of spiritual abandonment or divine withdrawal
Term names the inner act of departing from God, his ways, or moral uprightness — the turning aside of the heart away from covenantal faithfulness, or the refusal to depart from sinful patterns
Term names the departure of inner strength or spiritual endowment — the loss of an inward empowerment that sustained the person's identity and capacity
Term describes inner moral degeneration — the corruption of a people's inner nature manifesting as turning away from God and spiritual fruitlessness
Term names flax as the object of misplaced inner desire (spiritual unfaithfulness) or as the material worked with willing hands, expressing inner character through relationship to material provision

Term names the king's food as the occasion for inner moral decision — the choice not to defile oneself expresses inner conscience and moral resolve, and the food's association with loyalty makes it a test of inner character
Term names the wick or flax as a metaphor for the fragile or defeated person — in which divine compassion toward the vulnerable is expressed
Term in Isa 3:17 appears in a judgment oracle bringing shame and exposure to the daughters of Zion — the inner-being dimension is the shame and humiliation of public stripping
Term names the edict or judgment as something that shapes inner disposition — delay in executing judgment hardens the heart toward evil; promulgation of decree intends inner response
Term names the interpretive capacity as an expression of inner wisdom — the ability to discern meaning is a quality of the inner person whose wisdom transforms even outward bearing
Term names rebelliousness as an entrenched inner character trait — a deeply embedded disposition of perversity attributed to lineage and expressed in relational shame
Term names stubbornness of heart — the inner condition of wilful self-determination that refuses God's word and follows its own evil impulse, the defining characterisation of Israel's disobedience
Term names stubbornness as an inner condition of hardened resistance to God — the refusal to yield, named alongside wickedness and sin as a spiritual failure
Weakness as the inner-being condition of limitation, vulnerability, and human frailty — the site of divine power
Weakness and power in tension — the body's present weakness versus resurrection power; Christ's weakness as the pattern
"The spirit is willing but the flesh is weak" — the inner-being's aspiration limited by the weakness of the flesh
Weak in conscience — vulnerability of the inner moral faculty
Human weakness as the condition of grace — God chooses, Christ dies for, the weak
Weakness in faith and conscience — the inner-being condition of limited conviction or vulnerable moral formation
Weakness as the inner-being posture Paul embraces — solidarity, paradox, and power through weakness
The failings of the weak — inner-being scruples to be borne in community
Divine strengthening of the inner being after suffering — God restores what weakness has depleted
Term names slothfulness as an inner disposition that produces stupor and material ruin — inaction consuming the person from within
Term names the sluggard as a person whose inner desire craves but whose will refuses to act — passivity, inertia, and self-defeating inner contradiction
Term names the sluggard's inner self-deception — rationalisation and inflated self-regard enabling continued avoidance
Term names the inner collapse of courage and resolve — feeble hands, fainting spirit, melting heart in the face of threat or judgment
Term expresses divine faithfulness as the refusal to forsake — the assurance that addresses the inner fear of abandonment
Term describes the inner act of releasing, ceasing, or becoming still — letting go of wrath, being still before God, holding or releasing what matters
Term names the inner disposition of slackness and procrastination — the failure to act that is morally equivalent to destruction
Term names the inner disposition of laziness and reluctance — contrasted with zealous, fervent inner engagement
Term names sluggishness as the inner disposition of hesitation that must be overcome in pursuing God's purpose
Term names idleness as an inner disposition that the person of virtue refuses — its absence marking inner diligence and character
Term names the inner disposition of delay and hesitation — the reluctance to act that must be overcome in urgent obedience
